name: Nicotinic Acid
summary: Nicotinic acid is a vitamin B3 form with strong lipid effects but limited cardiovascular outcome benefit when added to statins. Safety concerns include flushing, hepatotoxicity, hyperglycemia, and gout.
description: GPR109A-activating niacin form with lipid-lowering effects; clinical outcome benefit is constrained by modern RCT results.
safetyNotes: Flushing; hepatotoxicity at high doses; hyperglycemia; gout risk; clinical-dose use requires medical context.
